clayVillageProfiles.name: Maya
clayVillageProfiles.bio: Fell in love with wheel throwing five years ago. There is nothing like the feeling of a bowl taking shape under your hands.
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: Meditative focus — my time at the wheel is when everything else fades away
clayVillageProfiles.currentWork: A set of textured stoneware bowls with carved surface patterns
clayVillageProfiles.feedbackPreference: Direct and honest, especially about form and proportion
clayVillageProfiles.glazeInterests: ash glazes, layered glazes for depth

clayVillageProfiles.name: James
clayVillageProfiles.bio: Twelve years of dedicated tea ware. A well-made teapot is one of the most challenging and rewarding forms.
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: The intersection of engineering and art — every spout, lid, and handle is a puzzle
clayVillageProfiles.currentWork: A series of side-handle teapots inspired by Japanese kyusu
clayVillageProfiles.feedbackPreference: Technical feedback on function — does the spout pour cleanly?

guidanceOffers.title: Live Throwing Session
guidanceOffers.desc: One-on-one live video session where we work on your throwing or trimming technique in real time.

guidanceOffers.title: Monthly Trimming Guidance
guidanceOffers.desc: Ongoing monthly guidance with weekly check-ins on your trimming practice.

guidanceOffers.title: Trimming Critique
guidanceOffers.desc: Send photos and videos of your trimming. I'll assess wall thickness, foot ring quality, and overall form.

clayVillageProfiles.teachingStyle: Direct and practical. Clear goals, honest feedback. I care more about your spout than your feelings.

clayVillageProfiles.name: Fox
clayVillageProfiles.bio: The friendly fox has been greeting visitors to Clay Village for years. Always eager to help newcomers find their way around and make their first cup of tea.
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: Bringing people together over shared craft
clayVillageProfiles.currentWork: Welcome mugs for every new villager
clayVillageProfiles.feedbackPreference: Warm encouragement first, gentle suggestions second
clayVillageProfiles.teachingStyle: Patient and encouraging. I meet you where you are, not where I think you should be.

clayVillageProfiles.name: Owl
clayVillageProfiles.bio: The wise owl tends the village library with meticulous care, cataloguing ceramic techniques from every tradition and era.
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: Preserving centuries of ceramic wisdom for the next generation
clayVillageProfiles.currentWork: A comprehensive glaze recipe archive organized by firing temperature

guidanceOffers.title: Historical Technique Session
guidanceOffers.desc: An hour exploring the origins and lineage of any technique you're working with. Context makes craft meaningful.

guidanceOffers.title: Glaze Archive Consultation
guidanceOffers.desc: I'll recommend glaze recipes and historical resources from the archive for whatever you're trying to achieve.

clayVillageProfiles.teachingStyle: Historical and contextual. Every technique has a lineage — I teach you where it came from before I show you how to do it.

clayVillageProfiles.name: Crane
clayVillageProfiles.bio: Patient and graceful, the crane guides potters through their creative journey. Every apprentice finds their own path under the crane's steady wing.
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: The patience to shape something beautiful, one careful movement at a time
clayVillageProfiles.currentWork: Pairing new apprentices with experienced mentors across the village
clayVillageProfiles.feedbackPreference: Patient, observational — watches your process before offering thoughts

guidanceOffers.title: Milestone Review
guidanceOffers.desc: Submit your milestone work for formal review and approval on any Mastery Path.

guidanceOffers.title: Path Consultation
guidanceOffers.desc: A quiet conversation about where you are, where you want to go, and what your next step should be.

clayVillageProfiles.teachingStyle: I watch first, speak second. I will not interrupt your process. When I do speak, listen carefully.

clayVillageProfiles.name: Cat
clayVillageProfiles.bio: The cat maintains the village studios with quiet care, purring contentedly while potters work. A calm presence that makes every studio feel like home.
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: Quiet meditation in the making — the hum of the wheel, the warmth of the kiln
clayVillageProfiles.currentWork: Small figurines and abstract sculpture for the studio shelves
clayVillageProfiles.feedbackPreference: Quiet nod of approval, or a gentle paw on your arm if something needs attention

clayVillageProfiles.name: Deer
clayVillageProfiles.bio: Graceful and welcoming, the deer brings visiting artists from far away to share in the village craft. Every culture has something to teach about clay.
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: Connecting potters across borders — clay speaks every language
clayVillageProfiles.currentWork: Coordinating the spring artist exchange with potters from three continents
clayVillageProfiles.feedbackPreference: Cross-cultural perspective — how does this technique connect to other traditions?
clayVillageProfiles.currentFocus: Bringing potters from around the world

clayVillageProfiles.name: Dog
clayVillageProfiles.bio: Energetic and friendly, the dog keeps the bonfire warm and organizes the best community gatherings. Every Friday is firing night!
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: Gathering around the fire to celebrate the work we have done together
clayVillageProfiles.currentWork: Planning the next bonfire pit-firing party with platters for everyone
clayVillageProfiles.feedbackPreference: Enthusiastic and supportive — every piece is worth celebrating

clayVillageProfiles.name: You
clayVillageProfiles.bio: You created Clay Village. Manage everything from here.
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: Building a world where potters can gather, learn, and create together

clayVillageProfiles.name: Ben
clayVillageProfiles.bio: Building Clay Village — a place for potters to gather, not perform. Also building the X reply app I never released because it made the internet faker.
clayVillageProfiles.whatClayMeans: Making something real in a world full of noise

insights: Closing statement: "Don't build a platform that hides people. That's not protection. That's a cage."

gap.does: Refreshes the like count every 3 minutes

insights: Closing statement: "Let me own my presence. Let me take it with me if I leave. That's the only thing that matters."

gap.says: I'll never trust a platform again
gap.does: Is here at this table — which means she still hopes

insights: Closing statement: "I don't need to make anything. I just need to be near people who do."

gap.says: I don't know what's real anymore
gap.does: Already knows — she just described kintsugi without knowing the word

insights: Closing statement: "Build the infrastructure. Leave the culture alone."

gap.does: His tradition is the most sophisticated design system at this table — it just predates the digital world

insights: Closing statement: "Build something that lets him keep making without me worrying every month."

gap.does: She already knows the work matters — the gap is between meaning and money

insights: Closing statement: "Build something that sees me. That's all I ask."

gap.says: I just need students to find me
gap.does: Has created something studios can't — she just doesn't know how to scale the intimacy

insights: Closing statement: "I need to see it working for someone like me before I trust it."

gap.does: The work literally improved when he stopped performing — the evidence is in the clay

insights: Closing statement: "I'm not a pottery person. I just know what I love when I hold it."

gap.says: I want more things like this
gap.does: Already has a deep emotional relationship with craft — just doesn't have the language for it

insights: Closing statement: "Can the village do that? Just... help me find someone who'll show me what I'm doing wrong?"

gap.does: Already has the most important thing — she can't stop

sofia.delta: Revisited. Core beliefs held steady. Sofia is consistent.

insights: Closing statement: "I hope you're the one that proves me wrong. I really do. But I've hoped before."

gap.says: I've seen too many good ideas corrupted
gap.does: Already knows what making without metrics feels like — he just left it behind for decades

insights: Closing statement: "Is that so complicated? The money goes to the maker. That's it."

gap.does: Can't even claim her identity because the economics won't let her

insights: Closing statement: "I'm not against what you're building. I'm against risking what I've already built. Come back with traffic."

gap.does: Has optimised the art out of his own process

insights: Closing statement: "Build what you described. I'm already your customer. I've been waiting for this."

gap.does: Exactly that — she is the customer who already exists

insights: Closing statement: "I've seen this movie before. Prove me wrong. I genuinely hope you can."

gap.says: Humans are tribal and status-seeking by nature
gap.does: His most meaningful community has no metrics at all

insights: Closing statement: "Come back when you have a booking system. Then we'll talk about philosophy."

gap.does: Her studio already IS a community — she just calls it a business

insights: Closing statement: "I respect the vision. But vision doesn't pay back a Series A. Show me the model."

gap.does: His most valued relationships are with small, intimate businesses

marcus.delta: Revisited. Core beliefs held steady. Marcus is consistent.

insights: Closing statement: "Build for the work. Not for the numbers around the work."

gap.does: Remembers every serious collector who ever held his work

insights: Closing statement: "Build it. But don't pretend it's easy. And don't pretend the people at Instagram are evil. Most of us are just tired."

gap.does: Hasn't been proud of her work in years

insights: Closing statement: "If the village makes room for people who just make — not sell, not teach, not perform — then you've built something real."

gap.does: Has built exactly what the village promises — gift economy, real relationships, making for joy — without any platform

insights: Closing statement: "The hand always finishes what the machine starts. That's enough."

gap.says: I don't care if it's cheating
gap.does: The part he's proudest of is the part AI can't do

insights: Closing statement: "Don't take outside money until you know who you are. And charge from day one. Free platforms attract the wrong crowd."

gap.does: Her analysis of why it failed is the blueprint for making it work

insights: Closing statement: "Every generation forgets the makers of the previous generation. If your platform remembers — actually remembers — that alone justifies its existence."

gap.does: Haunted by the makers she didn't find in time
